channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Importance of Ministerial and MEC Accountability in South Africa’s Governance
item.description: Since the dawn of democracy in 1994, South Africa has faced the challenge of building credible and accountable institutions. Despite the progress made, the absence of Ministers and Members of the Exec...

item.title: Tinubu Acknowledges Fuel Price Hardship, Highlights Nigeria’s Relative Position
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has addressed the growing concerns among Nigerians regarding the rising cost of fuel and its impact on their livelihoods. During a visit to Bayelsa State, where he inaugurated se...

item.title: Authorities Investigating Major Bank Card Fraud in Tanzania Involving Sh148 Billion
item.description: Tanzanian authorities have launched investigations into a massive Sh147.5 billion bank card fraud, which occurred during the 2024/25 financial year. The fraud involved the use of fake or cloned bank c...

item.title: Rivers Chief of Staff Celebrated by His Maternal Family for Service to State
item.description: Hon. Barr. Sonny Ewule, the Chief of Staff to the Rivers State Governor, was warmly celebrated by his maternal family, the Obinna family in Emohua Local Government Area, during a special Easter Monday...

item.title: Midnight Phantom: Nadja's Growing Affection for Brandon in Chapter Nine
item.description: In the latest chapter of 'Midnight Phantom,' Nadja's relationship with Brandon takes a significant turn as she begins to develop real feelings for him. This shift marks a pivotal moment in their compl...

item.title: DSWD announces special cash aid payout for unlisted jeepney and tricycle drivers in Metro Manila
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) will begin a special payout next week for public utility jeepney (PUJ) drivers and tricycle operators and drivers’ association (TODA) members wh...

item.title: KCMC aims to raise Sh2.6 billion to complete new cardiac care centre
item.description: Kilimanjaro Christian Medical Centre (KCMC) is requesting Sh2.6 billion to finalize the construction of a modern cardiovascular treatment centre. The facility, once completed, will help improve heart ...

item.title: Deputy President Kindiki offers apology to MP Kiaraho’s family after remarks about his death
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has offered a heartfelt apology to the family of the late Nyandarua Member of Parliament, John Kiaraho, following comments he made earlier that were deemed insensitive...

item.title: Childhood Immunization Rates Decline in B.C. Since Pandemic, Experts Warn
item.description: Childhood immunization rates in British Columbia have been dropping since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic, raising concerns among public health officials about the risk of infectious disease outbre...

item.title: INEC delay nationwide voter revalidation till after 2027 election
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don announce say dem go postpone the planned nationwide voter revalidation exercise until after the 2027 general elections. Mr Mohammed Haruna, wey...

item.title: Trump administration submits official plans for proposed 250-foot monument near Lincoln Memorial
item.description: The Trump administration has presented new architectural renderings for a proposed 250-foot triumphal arch intended to become one of the most striking landmarks in Washington, D.C. The elaborate desig...

item.title: Ukraine and Russia announce temporary Easter ceasefire amid calls for renewed peace talks
item.description: Ukraine and Russia have agreed to a 32-hour ceasefire over the Orthodox Easter weekend, marking a rare pause in the ongoing war. The truce, announced by Vladimir Putin, begins on Saturday afternoon an...

item.title: Pawan Khera granted pre-arrest bail in case over remarks about Himanta Sarma's wife
item.description: Pawan Khera, a prominent Congress leader, was granted pre-arrest bail by the Gauhati High Court in a case related to his controversial remarks about the wife of Himanta Biswa Sarma, the Chief Minister...

item.title: Deputy Speaker Calls for Stronger Ethanol Regulations to Tackle Illicit Alcohol Trade
item.description: Nairobi — Deputy Speaker Gladys Boss has formally petitioned the Public Petitions Committee to tighten regulations on ethanol, which is commonly abused in making illegal brews across Kenya. She highli...

item.title: Tensions rise as early breaches test US-Iran ceasefire agreement
item.description: A delicate ceasefire between the United States and Iran, announced only two days ago, is already showing signs of unraveling amid accusations of violations from both sides. Israel has conducted its mo...

item.title: Understanding the Decline in Reading Habits Among Indians
item.description: In a recent episode of Scroll Adda, books editor Arunava Sinha, who has translated over 100 books from Bengali to English, shared his insights on the state of reading and publishing in India. Sinha di...

item.title: African charity sues Prince Harry following resignation as patron
item.description: Prince Harry is facing legal action from Sentebale, a charity he co-founded in Africa in memory of his late mother, Princess Diana. The organisation, which provides support to young people living with...

item.title: Human Rights Commission staff left upset after controversial presentation
item.description: In March 2026, Queensland Human Rights Commission staff attended an office planning day, which turned into a highly distressing experience for many. The event featured a presentation by Anne Macdonald...

item.title: Police arrest two youths for spreading false claims about genital theft in Mwanza
item.description: Police in Mwanza have detained two young men after they falsely claimed that their genitals were stolen, creating panic among local communities. The first incident happened on April 6, 2026, when 18-y...

item.title: Allegations of Nazi-Like Behavior and Calls for Desnazification in Israeli-Palestinian Conflict
